continuity_rules: Mio never casts a shadow.
Ren's cracked sword guard is visible in every close-up.
Lantern light is the only soft glow in the ruined city.
story_bible.logline: A wandering swordsman escorts a silent ghost child through a city that remembers every war it lost.
story_bible.main_conflict: Ren must protect Mio without letting fear of loss turn him back into the weapon he used to be.
story_bible.synopsis: In the ruined city of Karakuri Vale, swordsman Ren Aki survives by avoiding attachments. When he finds Mio, a ghost child carrying the last lantern of the city, he is forced to guide her across collapsed districts while ash spirits and old war machines hunt the light she protects.
world_rules: Ghosts remain visible only near lantern light.
Broken war machines awaken when they hear drawn steel.
The ruined city rearranges streets around unresolved memories.

continuity_rules: Mio never casts a shadow.
Ren's cracked sword guard is visible in every close-up.
Lantern light is the only soft glow in the ruined city.
story_bible.logline: A wandering swordsman escorts a silent ghost child through a city that remembers every war it lost.
story_bible.main_conflict: Ren must protect Mio without letting fear of loss turn him back into the weapon he used to be.
story_bible.synopsis: In the ruined city of Karakuri Vale, swordsman Ren Aki survives by avoiding attachments. When he finds Mio, a ghost child carrying the last lantern of the city, he is forced to guide her across collapsed districts while ash spirits and old war machines hunt the light she protects.
world_rules: Ghosts remain visible only near lantern light.
Broken war machines awaken when they hear drawn steel.
The ruined city rearranges streets around unresolved memories.

key_objects.description: A rain-spotted paper lantern that keeps Mio visible and holds the last warm light in the city.
key_objects.name: Mio's Lantern
key_objects.significance: The lantern is the reason ash spirits pursue Mio and the reason Karakuri Vale can still remember hope.
key_objects.visual_notes: Handmade paper sides, bent bamboo frame, small flame that does not flicker in rain.

locations.description: A rain-slick ruined city of collapsed arcades, shrine gates, hanging wires, and dormant war machines.
locations.name: Karakuri Vale
rules: The city shifts at dawn.
Ash spirits cannot cross a lit threshold.
locations.visual_notes: Broken rooflines, deep black alleys, white mist, lantern reflections in puddles.

previous_summaries.prompt: Ghost child with lantern, no shadow beneath her feet.
previous_summaries.summary: Mio floats out from the lantern light without a shadow.
Ren crosses the ruined city and discovers Mio's lantern in the rubble.

chapter.goal: Move Ren from isolation into reluctant guardianship.
chapter.summary: Ren discovers Mio in the ruins and chooses to protect her from ash spirits crossing the city.
chapter.title: The Lantern in the Rubble

page_plan.summary: Mio appears and ash spirits begin to gather.

panel_plan.story_beat: Ash spirits crawl down the cracked walls toward the lantern.
panel_plan.visual_notes: Black ash hands on walls, Ren half-draws sword.

scene.summary: Ren crosses the dead city alone and hears a child humming in the rubble.
scene.title: Rain Over Karakuri Vale

chapter_outline.summary: Ren finds Mio and chooses to cross the city rather than abandon her.
chapter_outline.title: The Lantern in the Rubble

continuity_rules: Mio never casts a shadow.
Ren's cracked sword guard is visible in every close-up.
Lantern light is the only soft glow in the ruined city.
story_bible.logline: A wandering swordsman escorts a silent ghost child through a city that remembers every war it lost.
story_bible.main_conflict: Ren must protect Mio without letting fear of loss turn him back into the weapon he used to be.
story_bible.synopsis: In the ruined city of Karakuri Vale, swordsman Ren Aki survives by avoiding attachments. When he finds Mio, a ghost child carrying the last lantern of the city, he is forced to guide her across collapsed districts while ash spirits and old war machines hunt the light she protects.
story_bible.target_audience: Teen and young adult manga readers
world_rules: Ghosts remain visible only near lantern light.
Broken war machines awaken when they hear drawn steel.
The ruined city rearranges streets around unresolved memories.

story_bible_summary.main_conflict: Ren must protect Mio without letting fear of loss turn him back into the weapon he used to be.
story_bible_summary.synopsis: In the ruined city of Karakuri Vale, swordsman Ren Aki survives by avoiding attachments. When he finds Mio, a ghost child carrying the last lantern of the city, he is forced to guide her across collapsed districts while ash spirits and old war machines hunt the light she protects.

key_objects.description: A rain-spotted paper lantern that keeps Mio visible and holds the last warm light in the city.
key_objects.name: Mio's Lantern
key_objects.significance: The lantern is the reason ash spirits pursue Mio and the reason Karakuri Vale can still remember hope.
key_objects.visual_notes: Handmade paper sides, bent bamboo frame, small flame that does not flicker in rain.

locations.description: A rain-slick ruined city of collapsed arcades, shrine gates, hanging wires, and dormant war machines.
locations.name: Karakuri Vale
rules: The city shifts at dawn.
Ash spirits cannot cross a lit threshold.
locations.visual_notes: Broken rooflines, deep black alleys, white mist, lantern reflections in puddles.

previous_summaries.prompt: Sword arc splits black ash, speed lines, rain spray.
previous_summaries.summary: Ren cuts through ash without looking back.
Mio appears and ash spirits begin to gather.
Ren crosses the ruined city and discovers Mio's lantern in the rubble.

chapter.goal: Move Ren from isolation into reluctant guardianship.
chapter.summary: Ren discovers Mio in the ruins and chooses to protect her from ash spirits crossing the city.
chapter.title: The Lantern in the Rubble

page_plan.summary: Ren fights the spirits while Mio protects the lantern.

panel_plan.story_beat: Mio hugs the lantern as the city stairs shift open.
panel_plan.visual_notes: Stairway forms from rubble behind Mio.

scene.summary: Ren crosses the dead city alone and hears a child humming in the rubble.
scene.title: Rain Over Karakuri Vale

chapter_outline.summary: Ren finds Mio and chooses to cross the city rather than abandon her.
chapter_outline.title: The Lantern in the Rubble

continuity_rules: Mio never casts a shadow.
Ren's cracked sword guard is visible in every close-up.
Lantern light is the only soft glow in the ruined city.
story_bible.logline: A wandering swordsman escorts a silent ghost child through a city that remembers every war it lost.
story_bible.main_conflict: Ren must protect Mio without letting fear of loss turn him back into the weapon he used to be.
story_bible.synopsis: In the ruined city of Karakuri Vale, swordsman Ren Aki survives by avoiding attachments. When he finds Mio, a ghost child carrying the last lantern of the city, he is forced to guide her across collapsed districts while ash spirits and old war machines hunt the light she protects.
story_bible.target_audience: Teen and young adult manga readers
world_rules: Ghosts remain visible only near lantern light.
Broken war machines awaken when they hear drawn steel.
The ruined city rearranges streets around unresolved memories.

story_bible_summary.main_conflict: Ren must protect Mio without letting fear of loss turn him back into the weapon he used to be.
story_bible_summary.synopsis: In the ruined city of Karakuri Vale, swordsman Ren Aki survives by avoiding attachments. When he finds Mio, a ghost child carrying the last lantern of the city, he is forced to guide her across collapsed districts while ash spirits and old war machines hunt the light she protects.

continuity_rules: Mio never casts a shadow.
Ren's cracked sword guard is visible in every close-up.
Lantern light is the only soft glow in the ruined city.
story_bible.logline: A wandering swordsman escorts a silent ghost child through a city that remembers every war it lost.
story_bible.main_conflict: Ren must protect Mio without letting fear of loss turn him back into the weapon he used to be.
story_bible.synopsis: In the ruined city of Karakuri Vale, swordsman Ren Aki survives by avoiding attachments. When he finds Mio, a ghost child carrying the last lantern of the city, he is forced to guide her across collapsed districts while ash spirits and old war machines hunt the light she protects.
world_rules: Ghosts remain visible only near lantern light.
Broken war machines awaken when they hear drawn steel.
The ruined city rearranges streets around unresolved memories.

key_objects.description: A rain-spotted paper lantern that keeps Mio visible and holds the last warm light in the city.
key_objects.name: Mio's Lantern
key_objects.significance: The lantern is the reason ash spirits pursue Mio and the reason Karakuri Vale can still remember hope.
key_objects.visual_notes: Handmade paper sides, bent bamboo frame, small flame that does not flicker in rain.

locations.description: A rain-slick ruined city of collapsed arcades, shrine gates, hanging wires, and dormant war machines.
locations.name: Karakuri Vale
rules: The city shifts at dawn.
Ash spirits cannot cross a lit threshold.
locations.visual_notes: Broken rooflines, deep black alleys, white mist, lantern reflections in puddles.

previous_summaries.prompt: Hands repairing paper lantern, sword resting nearby.
previous_summaries.summary: Ren kneels and repairs the lantern handle with his scarf thread.
Ren fights the spirits while Mio protects the lantern.
Mio appears and ash spirits begin to gather.

chapter.goal: Move Ren from isolation into reluctant guardianship.
chapter.summary: Ren discovers Mio in the ruins and chooses to protect her from ash spirits crossing the city.
chapter.title: The Lantern in the Rubble

page_plan.summary: Ren accepts the burden of escorting Mio through the city.

panel_plan.narration: For the first time in years, Ren did not walk alone.
panel_plan.story_beat: Ren and Mio leave together under the last lantern glow.
panel_plan.visual_notes: Two silhouettes on broken bridge, lantern glow in rain.

scene.summary: Ren crosses the dead city alone and hears a child humming in the rubble.
scene.title: Rain Over Karakuri Vale

chapter_outline.summary: Ren finds Mio and chooses to cross the city rather than abandon her.
chapter_outline.title: The Lantern in the Rubble

continuity_rules: Mio never casts a shadow.
Ren's cracked sword guard is visible in every close-up.
Lantern light is the only soft glow in the ruined city.
story_bible.logline: A wandering swordsman escorts a silent ghost child through a city that remembers every war it lost.
story_bible.main_conflict: Ren must protect Mio without letting fear of loss turn him back into the weapon he used to be.
story_bible.synopsis: In the ruined city of Karakuri Vale, swordsman Ren Aki survives by avoiding attachments. When he finds Mio, a ghost child carrying the last lantern of the city, he is forced to guide her across collapsed districts while ash spirits and old war machines hunt the light she protects.
story_bible.target_audience: Teen and young adult manga readers
world_rules: Ghosts remain visible only near lantern light.
Broken war machines awaken when they hear drawn steel.
The ruined city rearranges streets around unresolved memories.
